title: Ranunculus deformis
intro: Ranunculus deformis, also known as Deformed Buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and lobed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which may aid in wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus deformis include ranunculin, a toxic alkaloid responsible for its medicinal and pharmacological effects, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. In herbal medicine, Ranunculus deformis is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or salve, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including eczema, acne, and ulcers, although its use should be approached with caution due to the potential toxicity of its alkaloids.
taxonomy: Ranunculus deformis is classified within the family Ranunculaceae. It belongs to the genus Ranunculus, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as buttercups. The Ranunculaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants with over 2,000 species. Ranunculus deformis is a species of flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is a perennial herb with yellow flowers.
uses: Ranunculus deformis is native to the southwestern United States, but limited information is available about its medicinal uses. However, various species of Ranunculus have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties. Some species have been used to treat fever, wounds, and respiratory issues due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. More research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Ranunculus deformis specifically. Traditional herbalists often use caution when handling plants in the Ranunculus genus due to their potential toxicity.

side_effects: Ranunculus deformis is known to be highly toxic, and ingestion of its plant parts can lead to severe side effects. The plant's toxic compounds, including ranunculin, can cause cell lysis and damage to the gastrointestinal tract. Ingestion of Ranunculus deformis can result in symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. If ingested in large quantities, it can also lead to more severe complications, including kidney damage and respiratory failure. The plant's toxicity can be a concern for livestock and pets that may accidentally ingest its plant parts.
